channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested
item.description: The second iteration of the German-speaking online crime marketplace had over 22,000 users and more than 100 sellers. The post Resurrected ‘Crimenetwork’ Marketplace Taken Down, Administrator Arrested appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Issues passing Challenge Pages in China
item.description: May 11, 07:21 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.May 11, 05:33 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating reports of errors after users pass challenge pages in China. We are working to mitigate this problem.

item.title: GraphDC: A Divide-and-Conquer Multi-Agent System for Scalable Graph Algorithm Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2605.06671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have demonstrated strong potential for many mathematical problems. However, their performance on graph algorithmic tasks is still unsatisfying, since graphs are naturally more complex in topology and often require...

item.title: More Thinking, More Bias: Length-Driven Position Bias in Reasoning Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.06672v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning and reasoning-tuned models such as DeepSeek-R1 are commonly assumed to reduce shallow heuristic biases by thinking carefully. We test this on position bias in multiple-choice QA and find a different story: within any...

item.title: Fast and Effective Redistricting Optimization via Composite-Move Tabu Search
item.description: arXiv:2605.06682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Spatial redistricting is a practical combinatorial optimization problem that demands high-quality solutions, rapid turnaround, and flexibility to accommodate multi-criteria objectives and interactive refinement. A central challenge is the contiguity...

item.title: State Representation and Termination for Recursive Reasoning Systems
item.description: arXiv:2605.06690v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recursive reasoning systems alternate between acquiring new evidence and refining an accumulated understanding. Two design choices are typically left implicit: how to represent the evolving reasoning state, and when to stop iterating. This paper...

item.title: Hidden Coalitions in Multi-Agent AI: A Spectral Diagnostic from Internal Representations
item.description: arXiv:2605.06696v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Collections of interacting AI agents can form coalitions, creating emergent group-level organization that is critical for AI safety and alignment. However, observing agent behavior alone is often insufficient to distinguish genuine informational...

item.title: RateQuant: Optimal Mixed-Precision KV Cache Quantization via Rate-Distortion Theory
item.description: arXiv:2605.06675v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models cache all previously computed key-value (KV) pairs during generation, and this KV cache grows linearly with sequence length, making it a primary memory bottleneck for serving. Quantizing the KV cache to fewer bits reduces this...

item.title: LKV: End-to-End Learning of Head-wise Budgets and Token Selection for LLM KV Cache Eviction
item.description: arXiv:2605.06676v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Long-context inference in Large Language Models (LLMs) is bottlenecked by the linear growth of Key-Value (KV) cache memory. Existing KV cache compression paradigms are fundamentally limited by heuristics: heuristic budgeting relies on statistical...

item.title: A Wasserstein GAN-based climate scenario generator for risk management and insurance: the case of soil subsidence
item.description: arXiv:2605.06678v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: According to the United Nations Office for Disaster Risk Reduction (2025), the average annual cost of natural catastrophes increased from 70--80 billion USD between 1970 and 2000 to 180--200 billion USD between 2001 and 2020. Reports from...

item.title: Breaking the Illusion: When Positive Meets Negative in Multimodal Decoding
item.description: arXiv:2605.06679v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Vision-Language Models (VLMs) are frequently undermined by object hallucination, generating content that contradicts visual reality, due to an over-reliance on linguistic priors. We introduce Positive-and-Negative Decoding (PND), a training-free...

item.title: On the Role of Strain and Vorticity in Numerical Integration Error for Flow Matching
item.description: arXiv:2605.06680v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Flow matching generates data by integrating a learned velocity field, where the number of integration steps (NFE) directly determines inference cost. We analyze which properties of the velocity field govern integration error by decomposing the...

item.title: Domain-level metacognitive monitoring in frontier LLMs: A 33-model atlas
item.description: arXiv:2605.06673v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Aggregate metacognitive quality scores mask within-model variation across MMLU benchmark domains. We administered 1,500 MMLU items (250 per domain, under an a priori six-domain grouping) to 33 frontier LLMs from eight model families and computed...

item.title: VITA-QinYu: Expressive Spoken Language Model for Role-Playing and Singing
item.description: arXiv:2605.06765v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Human speech conveys expressiveness beyond linguistic content, including personality, mood, or performance elements, such as a comforting tone or humming a song, which we formalize as role-playing and singing. We present VITA-QinYu, the first...

item.title: IntentGrasp: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Intent Understanding
item.description: arXiv:2605.06832v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Accurately understanding the intent behind speech, conversation, and writing is crucial to the development of helpful Large Language Model (LLM) assistants. This paper introduces IntentGrasp, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the intent...

item.title: TajPersLexon: A Tajik-Persian Lexical Resource and Hybrid Model for Cross-Script Low-Resource NLP
item.description: arXiv:2605.06886v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This work introduces TajPersLexon, a curated Tajik--Persian parallel lexical resource of 40,112 word and short-phrase pairs for cross-script lexical retrieval, transliteration, and alignment in low-resource settings. We conduct a comprehensive...

item.title: MIST: Multimodal Interactive Speech-based Tool-calling Conversational Assistants for Smart Homes
item.description: arXiv:2605.06897v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of Internet of Things (IoT) devices in the physical world necessitates voice-based interfaces capable of handling complex user experiences. While modern Large Language Models (LLMs) already demonstrate strong tool-usage capabilities, modeling...

item.title: Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign
item.description: Victims span across the aviation, critical infrastructure, energy, logistics, public administration, and technology sectors. The post Over 500 Organizations Hit in Years-Long Phishing Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: DeepSeek Seeks Up to $7.35B in First-Round Financing, Drawing Tencent, Alibaba, and State Fund Bidding War
item.description: DeepSeek seeks up to $7.35B in first-round financing with Tencent, Alibaba, and state funds in a bidding war, while founder Liang Wenfeng insists on keeping DeepSeek independent—a key test case for China's AI startup ecosystem.

item.title: Huawei Pura X Max Sells Out; Collector Edition Outshines Standard Model
item.description: Huawei's premium foldable Pura X Max sells out with collector's edition (55%) outselling standard model (45%), a rare countertrend in China's contracting smartphone market where the more expensive variant outperformed despite overall industry weakness.

item.title: CATL-Backed SENASIC Passes Hong Kong Hearing, Becomes China's Largest Auto Wireless SoC Company
item.description: CATL-backed SENASIC passes Hong Kong hearing, becoming China's largest auto wireless SoC company with CATL's rare three-round consecutive investment signaling major EV battery giants' push into automotive sensing chips.

item.title: Huawei HDC 2026 Set for June 12–14: HarmonyOS 7 Officially Confirmed
item.description: Huawei's annual developer conference returns June 12–14, with HarmonyOS 7 officially confirmed after months of speculation.

item.title: Xiaomi YU7 Entry-Level Version Spotted: 73 kWh Battery, 633 km Range
item.description: Xiaomi's YU7 lineup expands with a new entry-level variant featuring a 73 kWh battery and 633 km range, positioned as the most affordable model in the series.

item.title: Users may experience elevated errors in ChatGPT uploading files and Codex Cloud creating tasks
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components File uploads (Degraded performance) Codex Web (Operational)

item.title: MachinaCheck: Building a Multi-Agent CNC Manufacturability System on AMD MI300X
Network Performance Issues in Auckland
item.description: May 10, 17:54 UTCResolved - Cloudflare identified issues with network performance that occurred for our Auckland, NZ facility between 12:30 and 16:00 UTC today, 2026-05-10. Customers may have noticed increased latency for traffic handled by this facility during this period.

item.title: Ollama Out-of-Bounds Read Vulnerability Allows Remote Process Memory Leak
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed a critical security vulnerability in Ollama that, if successfully exploited, could allow a remote, unauthenticated attacker to leak its entire process memory. The out-of-bounds read flaw, which likely impacts over 300,000 servers globally, is tracked as...

item.title: Network Performance Issues
item.description: May 10, 03:28 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating issues with network performance in Chicago. We are working to analyse and mitigate this problem. More updates to follow shortly.

item.title: Claude Code on the Web Partial Outage
item.description: May 9, 23:51 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolvedMay 9, 23:33 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: "OncoAgent: A Dual-Tier Multi-Agent Framework for Privacy-Preserving Oncology Clinical Decision Support"
Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.1
item.description: May 9, 08:22 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 9, 07:57 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.

item.title: cPanel, WHM Release Fixes for Three New Vulnerabilities — Patch Now
item.description: cPanel has released updates to address three vulnerabilities in cPanel and Web Host Manager (WHM) that could be exploited to achieve privilege escalation, code execution, and denial-of-service. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-29201 (CVSS score: 4.3) - An insufficient input...

item.title: [AINews] Anthropic growing 10x/year while everyone else is laying off >10% of their workforce
item.description: A quiet day lets us reflect on an interesting dichotomy in the economy.

item.title: Claude Code IDE extension unable to load on Windows
item.description: May 9, 00:24 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved in v2.1.137. Please update to get the fix.May 9, 00:17 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and published in v2.1.137. We are monitoring results.May 8, 22:32 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being...

item.title: Elevated errors for Responses API
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered. Between 4:05pm and 4:40pm PT on May 8, some customers using the Responses API may have experienced elevated 404 errors. We identified the issue as related to a recent deploy and rolled it back. Responses API traffic has recovered, and...

item.title: Musk v. Altman week 2: OpenAI fires back, and Shivon Zilis reveals that Musk tried to poach Sam Altman
item.description: In the second week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk’s motivations for bringing the suit were under scrutiny. Last week, Musk took the stand, alleging that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into donating $38 million to the company. He claimed...

item.title: Certificate Issuance through Let's Encrypt unavailable
item.description: May 8, 20:58 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 8, 20:53 UTCMonitoring - We have confirmed recovery and will continue monitoring for improvement.May 8, 19:46 UTCInvestigating - We are aware of an issue affecting certificate issuance through Let's Encrypt. Customers using Universal...

item.title: TCLBANKER Banking Trojan Targets Financial Platforms via WhatsApp and Outlook Worms
item.description: Threat hunters have flagged a previously undocumented Brazilian banking trojan dubbed TCLBANKER that's capable of targeting 59 banking, fintech, and cryptocurrency platforms. The activity is being tracked by Elastic Security Labs under the moniker REF3076. The malware family is assessed to be a...

item.title: CyberSecQwen-4B: Why Defensive Cyber Needs Small, Specialized, Locally-Runnable Models
Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.7
item.description: May 8, 17:13 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.May 8, 17:01 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Here’s what you need to know about the cruise ship hantavirus outbreak
item.description: MIT Technology Review Explains: Let our writers untangle the complex, messy world of technology to help you understand what’s coming next. You can read more from the series here. Eight passengers aboard a Dutch-flagged cruise ship have contracted a type of hantavirus, a rare virus transmitted by...

item.title: Degraded Performance with Codex Cloud Tasks
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Codex Web (Operational) CLI (Operational) App (Operational) VS Code extension (Operational) Codex API (Operational)

item.title: EMO: Pretraining mixture of experts for emergent modularity
Elevated Errors on File Operations
item.description: May 8, 15:17 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.May 8, 14:12 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating this issue.

item.title: Fake Call History Apps Stole Payments From Users After 7.3 Million Play Store Downloads
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered fraudulent apps on the official Google Play Store for Android that falsely claimed to offer access to call histories for any phone number, only to trick users into joining a subscription that provided fake data and incurred financial loss. The 28 apps have...

item.title: In Other News: Train Hacker Arrested, PamDOORa Linux Backdoor, New CISA Director Frontrunner
item.description: Other noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: US gov targets 72-hour patch cycles, malware uses Windows Phone Link to steal OTPs, spy operation targets Eurasian drone industry. The post In Other News: Train Hacker Arrested, PamDOORa Linux Backdoor, New CISA Director Frontrunner...

item.title: Increased Error Rate for gpt-5.5 Model in the API
item.description: Status: IdentifiedWe implemented the mitigations and are observing reduction in error rates, but are still seeing higher than normal latency.Affected components Chat Completions (Degraded performance) Responses (Degraded performance)

item.title: Wrangler users may experience frequent log out
item.description: May 8, 12:52 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented, and we are monitoring the results.May 8, 10:36 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified, and we are working on a fixMay 8, 10:35 UTCInvestigating - We are currently investigating an issue where Wrangler users experience frequent log out.

item.title: The Download: AI malaise and babymaking tech
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. We’ve entered the era of AI malaise AI is spreading everywhere, and it is not going away. But what will it do? What effect will it have on our...

item.title: Polish Security Agency Reports ICS Breaches at Five Water Treatment Plants
item.description: The hackers gained the ability to modify equipment operational parameters, creating a direct risk to the public water supply. The post Polish Security Agency Reports ICS Breaches at Five Water Treatment Plants appeared first on SecurityWeek.
